description: Static validation: recurring due-work reader contains no compatibility merge with `client_billing_cycles` rows.
Integration: ready recurring work comes only from `recurring_service_periods` for a client-cadence recurring line.
Integration: ready recurring work comes only from `recurring_service_periods` for a contract-cadence recurring line.
Failure case: missing recurring service-period materialization raises an explicit repairable error instead of synthesizing a compatibility row.
Static validation: recurring due-work code no longer catches missing-table/missing-column errors as an accepted recurring fallback path.
Unit: client-cadence recurring due-work row identity can be built without `billingCycleId`.
Unit: recurring execution identity keys are stable and bridge-free for client cadence.
Unit: recurring execution identity keys are stable and bridge-free for contract cadence.
Static validation: shared recurring identity interfaces no longer require `billingCycleId` or `hasBillingCycleBridge`.
UI: `AutomaticInvoices` can render and act on a client-cadence recurring row whose bridge metadata is null.
UI: `AutomaticInvoices` can render and act on a contract-cadence recurring row without any billing-cycle bridge.
UI: recurring preview action sends canonical selector input only and no `billing_cycle_id`.
UI: recurring generate action sends canonical selector input only and no `billing_cycle_id`.
Integration: recurring preview endpoint rejects recurring requests that attempt to send `billing_cycle_id` as the recurring selector.
Integration: recurring generate endpoint rejects recurring requests that attempt to send `billing_cycle_id` as the recurring selector.
Integration: recurring preview succeeds for a client-cadence selector input with no billing-cycle bridge.
Integration: recurring preview succeeds for a contract-cadence selector input.
Integration: recurring generation succeeds for a client-cadence selector input with no billing-cycle bridge.
Integration: recurring generation succeeds for a contract-cadence selector input with no billing-cycle bridge.
Integration: duplicate prevention blocks rerunning the same client-cadence recurring window without consulting `invoices.billing_cycle_id`.
Integration: duplicate prevention blocks rerunning the same contract-cadence recurring window without consulting `invoices.billing_cycle_id`.
Static validation: duplicate-detection queries in recurring invoice generation do not prefer `invoices.billing_cycle_id`.
Unit: recurring retry/rerun payloads are keyed only by canonical recurring execution identity.
Integration: recurring run target selection uses only canonical due-work rows and does not call `getAvailableBillingPeriods(...)`.
Unit: recurring job payload schema for invoice generation does not require `billingCycleId`.
Integration: recurring invoice job handler executes a client-cadence recurring row using canonical selector identity only.
Integration: recurring invoice job handler executes a contract-cadence recurring row using canonical selector identity only.
Static validation: recurring run action names/contracts no longer accept raw `billingCycleIds` for recurring execution.
Integration: recurring invoice charge/detail linkage succeeds for a client-cadence invoice with null `billing_cycle_id`.
Integration: recurring invoice charge/detail linkage succeeds for a contract-cadence invoice.
Static validation: invoice-linkage code no longer widens or narrows matching rules based on `invoice.billing_cycle_id`.
Static validation: invoice-linkage code no longer contains mixed-schema fallback guards for recurring service-period relations.
Integration: recurring reverse/delete repairs recurring service-period linkage for a client-cadence invoice without mutating a billing-cycle primary object.
Integration: recurring reverse/delete repairs recurring service-period linkage for a contract-cadence invoice.
UI: recurring history row actions remain available for a recurring invoice whose bridge metadata is null.
UI: recurring history copy and labels refer to service periods/execution windows instead of billing cycles.
Static validation: billing-cycle-specific recurring history action names are removed or renamed.
Integration: invoice list/read classification derives recurring kind and cadence from canonical recurring summary data, not `invoices.billing_cycle_id`.
Integration: recurring invoice detail response includes canonical recurring periods even when billing-cycle metadata is absent.
Static validation: recurring invoice DTOs no longer require bridge-only recurring fields.
Static validation: recurring DTOs treat `billing_cycle_id` only as optional historical/client-context metadata.
Integration: recurring invoice read path does not infer client cadence simply because `billing_cycle_id` is present.
Integration: recurring invoice read path still surfaces passive billing-cycle metadata when historical invoices have it.
Static validation: recurring preview/generation error contracts no longer expose `billingCycleId` as the primary recurring diagnostic key.
Integration: recurring preview/generation errors map back to canonical execution-window identity only.
Integration: invoice modification/finalization does not classify a bridge-less recurring invoice as a prepayment.
Integration: true prepayment invoice behavior still works after explicit invoice-kind classification is introduced.
Static validation: recurring/prepayment classification logic no longer keys off null/non-null `billing_cycle_id`.
Integration: billing engine executes a client-cadence recurring invoice without loading or creating `client_billing_cycles` as recurring truth.
Integration: billing engine executes a contract-cadence recurring invoice without consulting `client_billing_cycles`.
Static validation: billing engine no longer auto-creates or loads cycle rows to execute recurring work.
Integration: selector-input recurring preview/generate rejects execution windows that do not match persisted recurring service periods.
Integration: client billing schedule change regenerates future recurring service periods beyond billed boundaries rather than defining future recurring work by mutating future cycle rows.
Static validation: client billing schedule management code remains available for legitimate client cadence administration.
Integration: bucket recurring period resolution follows canonical recurring service periods for client cadence.
Integration: bucket recurring period resolution follows canonical recurring service periods for contract cadence.
Integration: hourly recurring billing still bills content inside canonical service windows after bridge removal.
Integration: usage recurring billing still bills content inside canonical service windows after bridge removal.
Integration: accounting export uses canonical recurring detail periods for a client-cadence recurring invoice.
Integration: accounting export uses canonical recurring detail periods for a contract-cadence recurring invoice.
Integration: invoice lacking canonical recurring detail is exported as non-recurring/periodless rather than using recurring header fallback provenance.
Static validation: recurring accounting export no longer emits `invoice_header_fallback` or equivalent mixed canonical/fallback recurring provenance for live recurring paths.
UI: authoring still defaults `cadence_owner` explicitly if desired, but stored/runtime recurring behavior does not rely on unresolved client fallback semantics.
Static validation: rollout-era recurring authoring helpers no longer resolve unspecified cadence by implicit runtime fallback.
Static validation: recurring authoring/storage helpers no longer contain old-table fallback logic in normal read/write paths.
UI: recurring service-period management surface remains available for troubleshooting and repair after compatibility due-work rows are removed.
UI: missing recurring materialization is presented as an explicit repair action instead of a fallback-ready invoice row.
Static validation: recurring diagnostics/events no longer emit bridge-specific recurring payload fields.
Integration: recurring workflow or job events identify runs entirely by canonical recurring execution identity.
Static validation: recurring invoice queries no longer rely on billing-cycle bridge assumptions when summarizing service periods.
Static validation: only legitimate client billing schedule API schemas still expose billing-cycle-specific request contracts.
Static validation: shared package/server interfaces clearly separate client billing cycle models from recurring execution models.
Documentation: hard-cutover runbook explains the final recurring model and no longer describes compatibility due-work rows as normal behavior.
Documentation: final recurring architecture notes explain that `client_billing_cycles` remain cadence infrastructure only.
Design validation: the codebase has an explicit deprecation posture for `invoices.billing_cycle_id` in recurring code.
Integration: historical recurring invoice with incomplete canonical linkage remains readable without reintroducing live recurring bridge logic.
Regression: recurring client-cadence invoicing remains end-to-end functional after all live bridge assumptions are removed.
Regression: recurring contract-cadence invoicing remains end-to-end functional after all live bridge assumptions are removed.
Regression: recurring history, reverse, and reissue flows remain functional for client cadence after the hard cutover.
Regression: recurring history, reverse, and reissue flows remain functional for contract cadence after the hard cutover.
Regression: no live recurring path requires `billing_cycle_id` to discover, preview, generate, reverse, or explain recurring work.
Static validation: legacy recurring tests that only preserve compatibility fallback behavior are removed or rewritten.
DB-backed sanity: a migrated schema with required recurring-service-period structures is treated as mandatory, not optional, by recurring invoice code.
DB-backed sanity: client billing schedule records can still be managed independently of recurring invoice execution after the hard cutover.
DB-backed sanity: canonical recurring service-period linkage is sufficient to rebuild recurring invoice history for a bridge-less recurring invoice.
DB-backed sanity: deleting a recurring invoice reopens or repairs linked recurring service periods without mutating a billing-cycle object.
DB-backed sanity: changing a client cadence source rule regenerates future recurring service periods while leaving historical recurring invoices readable.
DB-backed sanity: a client-cadence recurring invoice can persist with null `billing_cycle_id` and still remain fully operable as recurring work.
DB-backed sanity: invoices that still carry `billing_cycle_id` remain readable as historical context without affecting recurring classification.
